title: Node 24, TypeScript 7, ESM, Dependencies, and Emitted Artifacts · Contract Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for node 24, typescript 7, esm, dependencies, and emitted artifacts and accepts only compiler plus runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reproducible source-to-emitted crawler repository. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Pin Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, ESM semantics, package manager, and supported platforms.
Pin Cheerio 1.2.0, robots-parser 3.0.1, p-limit 7.3.0, csv-stringify 6.8.1, Playwright 1.61.1, and axe-core 4.12.1 with provenance and license review.
Bind strict source, declarations, maps, generated fixtures, package exports, emitted JavaScript, and startup entry to one revision.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.text: A quiz TypeScript team handles case 1682 during an origin-fairness and event-loop capacity rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile Node, TypeScript, pinned crawler packages, emitted artifacts, clean gates, and recovery anchors; browser TypeScript remains deterministic and free of Node, network, filesystem, browser, and host effects; then change one owner permission, seed, URL, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, media, or encoding decision and reconcile provenance. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Node 24.18.0, TypeScript 7.0.2 and the TypeScript 6.0.2 compatibility API, ESM semantics, package manager, and supported platforms.
